entry.title: SAP Developer News January 8th,, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

CAP December 2025 release 

  • CAP December 2025 release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/dec25  
  • SAP Developer News episode covering Status-Transition Flows and the CAP November 2025 release https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BkCPoH7VKHg  
  • A simple exploration of status transition flows in CAP (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/08/a-simple-exploration-of-status-transition-flows-in-cap/  
  • Axiom AXI001 What not how https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/axioms/AXI001.md  
  • A new Hands-on SAP Dev mini-series on the core expression language in CDS (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/09/a-new-hands-on-sap-dev-mini-series-on-the-core-expression-language-in-cds/  
  • CDS expressions in CAP - Under the hood - Part 1 (upcoming live stream) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aiE20i5BP70  
  • Best Practice BES006 Compose with reuse in mind https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/bestpractices/BES006.md  
  • The OData "Common" Vocabulary's FieldControlType definitions https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.html#FieldControlType  

2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/2025-set-the-pace-2026-wins-the-race-abap-ai-with-joule-vs-code-and-ccm/ba-p/14302433 

What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

  • Overview: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-december-2025/ba-p/14295366 
  • New Machine Learning, NLP and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q4: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-machine-learning-nlp-and-ai-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2025-q4/ba-p/14293152 
  • SAP CodeJam on Foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud -- ML, Vectors, Knowledge Graph: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407#codejam1  

 Application Vulnerability Report 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-application-vulnerability-report-for-cloudfoundry-applications/ba-p/14281684 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 CAP December 2025 release 

6:20 2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

7:22 What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

9:36 Application Vulnerability Report 

10:32 Devtoberfest Winner Jose Pereira 

Transcript

00;00;00;08 - 00;00;05;26

This is the SAP Developer News for January 8th, 2026.

00;00;05;29 - 00;00;09;26

Over the holiday period, the CAP team delivered a nice present for us.

00;00;10;00 - 00;00;44;20

The December 2025 release. Let's take a quick look through the highlights. But before we do, let's check out the new FEATURE STATUS BADGES that are appearing more and more - in fact, there are a few in the notes for this release.

As we all know our Greek alphabet, we can correctly surmise that while BETA features are post-experimental and still possibly subject to change ... GAMMA features, not to be confused with the type of radiation that created The Incredible Hulk, are pretty much finalised and ready to use & test thoroughly.

00;00;44;22 - 00;01;19;15

Two such features that are at GAMMA level in this release are Status-Transition Flows and Declarative Constraints.

We covered Status-Transition Flows in a previous news item for the previous release, and there's an overview in this blog post we have for you.

So let's instead take a brief look at the super exciting Declarative Constraints. There are new possibilities with the @assert annotation to define complex constraints, in your CDS model.

00;01;19;18 - 00;01;50;01

That's right, declaratively. We should always strive to PUSH outwards, pushing logic up into the declarative model, and queries & data manipulation down to the database, which goes hand in hand with CAP’s axiom WHAT NOT HOW.

The conditions are expressed in another domain specific language in CDS - CXL - CDS Expression Language.

00;01;50;01 - 00;02;19;07

And, almost as if we'd planned it, there’s a new Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series on CXL starting Friday next week, with me and one of the fab members of the CAP compiler team, Patrice. Don't miss it!

CAP’s support for hierarchical tree views has just become a lot more streamlined, with a new @hierarchy annotation replacing a ton of OData specific ones.

00;02;19;08 - 00;02;46;26

Now that's what I call simple reuse, and cap is an incredibly important idea and a best practice to add in that context. There's a new command CDS export, with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service. Definitions. The output is a full featured cap reuse package that can be published to and pulled from npm registries.

00;02;46;29 - 00;03;15;23

Now that’s what I call simple.

Reuse in CAP is an incredibly important idea, and best practice too, and in that context, there’s a new command cds export with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service definitions; the output is a full-featured CAP reuse package that can be published to and pulled from NPM registries. If exporting via EDMX is the lossy MP3 audio format, then cds export gives us a lossless transfer, more like FLAC.

By the way, if you're interested to learn more about reuse in CAP, with NPM packages, we've a new blog post series Modules, modularity & reuse in CDS models which you might be interested in, with parts 1 and 2 already published.

00;03;15;26 - 00;03;45;29

In the CDS Language & Compiler topic, there's an enhancement that will appeal to those of you who develop and serve multiple frontends from the app/ directory. Now CDS file content from all ./app/* subdirectories will be fetched and loaded (say, with cds build or cds serve) instead of just from the app/ directory itself. This means we don't have to use an index.cds file in the app/ directory to import what we need.

00;03;46;01 - 00;04;19;26

Staying in the CDS Language & Compiler area, there's another enhancement that allows us to use symbols not only for enums defined in CDS, but now also for enums in OData annotations. The example in the release notes in Capire is great, because not only does it illustrate the enhancement, but it also causes the curious amongst us to use our brains and go to find out where the relation between 1 and Readonly, and between 7 and Mandatory, is defined.

00;04;19;26 - 00;04;51;01

And of course it's in OData's “Common” vocabulary, contributed by SAP.

In the Node.js runtime topic, it is now possible to make direct CRUD requests on draft-enabled entities; for example, instead of the EDIT-PATCH-SAVE sequence, you can now just send a direct UPDATE request.

Also, event queue processing has been consolidated into a default queue ... and custom queues, and there has been a change to the db-related guarantee, from ‘at least once’ to ‘exactly once’.

00;04;51;01 - 00;05;23;03

Related guarantee from at least once to exactly once. Finally, in the NodeJS runtime topic, there has been some cleanup of the model reflection APIs. Some function uses have been deprecated and the text entities are now available in a text property of the corresponding localized entity. Moving to the Java runtime topic, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft enabled entities.

00;05;23;06 - 00;05;52;09

Finally in the Node.js runtime topic, there has been some clean up of the model reflection APIs. Some function usages have been deprecated, and the .texts entities are now available in a texts property of the corresponding localised entity.

In the Java runtime, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft-enabled entities.

00;05;52;11 - 00;06;23;25

Now, as well as support for active entities, there’s also deep update support for inactive entities too, as long as the keys are included.

Additionally, there’s a new as(String alias) method on the CqnValue interface; with that, it’s possible to create aliased values directly, making it easier to build queries with aliased values.

In the Tools topic, there’s a Kyma convenience now for the cds up command; if your project contains Helm deployment resources, and no mta.yaml file, then you don't need to specify the --to k8s option explicitly any more.

And last but not least, in the Plugins topic, the Node.js plugin @Pa_Vi/cds-rfc now supports multitenancy.

There are links to everything I've mentioned, and more ... in the description.

00;06;23;27 - 00;06;45;04

In this blog post, Simona reflects on how 2025 laid the groundwork for AI powered ABAP development and gives us a clear line of sight into where things are going in 2026. One big highlight from the blogpost is that Joule for developers will remain free for another year, giving us more time to explore AI assisted coding in real projects.

00;06;45;07 - 00;07;15;00

The blog post walks us through the evolution of AI last year and what's to come. Smart tooling with the AI workflow, deeper integration with VS code, and a renowned focus on custom code migration using AI, all designed to help developers be more productive and modernize their code bases. And that's really the strategy going into 2020. Shipping early, scaling smart, and keeping the ABAP core clean in 2025 is about unlocking what I can do.

00;07;15;07 - 00;07;22;12

Then 2026 will make ABAP AI the core of AI about development.

00;07;22;15 - 00;07;54;05

The Q4 2025 release of SAP HANA Cloud brings many updates designed to streamline your workflow and unlock new possibilities. Let’s dive into what’s new.

Let's start with SAP HANA Cloud Central, which now supports background processing for long-running SQL statements that continue even after closing your browser. A new consolidated view of HDI schemas and containers provides quick access to HDI artifacts and their metadata.

00;07;54;08 - 00;08;36;26

SAP Business Data Cloud integration is streamlined—data products are automatically exposed as virtual tables, reducing manual setup and accelerating data reuse.

Calculation view modeling adds column lineage via SQL table functions, dynamic input parameters for MDS cube conversions, and runtime-evaluated restricted columns. Joins are enhanced with support for left outer joins in star joins.

This release introduces the ability to transform property graphs into semantic knowledge graphs without requiring redesign of data models, enabling flexible switching between paradigms on the same data for your use cases.

00;08;36;28 - 00;09;10;09

For developers and data scientists, AI and machine learning capabilities have undergone significant enhancements. The new Unified Time Series procedure provides a consistent interface across algorithms, simplifying development. Text embedding models have been improved to reduce output dimensions without sacrificing accuracy, and the new cross and convert model. In an NLP service ensures more precise search results. Ranking with the Hana Experiment Monitor user interface.

00;09;10;11 - 00;09;34;05

The final SAP CodeJam of 2025 took place in Istanbul, with a focus on AI in SAP HANA Cloud. The next one will happen in February in Berlin, and rest assured, it will be updated with some of these cool new enhancements. You can request this CodeJam topic in your location also!

00;09;34;08 - 00;09;38;22

Also see all the.

00;09;38;25 - 00;10;08;14

Why not start the year off right? With the cleanest, safest applications you can. That's what the new application vulnerability report service for on SAP. BTP focuses on Cloud Foundry applications. Is exactly for this new service. Now in beta will allow you to scan your running applications on SAP, BTP on a regular basis and look for critical vulnerabilities. Don't wait until something happens to one of your applications to find out that there's a vulnerability in it.

00;10;08;16 - 00;10;31;19

Be proactive and find out in advance. So I encourage you to check out this brand new service. I'll put the link to the blog post that describes it, as well as goes through the setup process. It's very easy to use and you can start getting your reports today, so I would encourage you to do so.

00;10;31;22 - 00;11;00;19

So my bedroom. So I hear that there were the two of us and have got a special leftovers right, to work with the community that long. You know what you want, right? Nope. There was kind of a learning journey that you did. Yeah. Our bosses always incentive us to look forward. And WhatsApp has to offer. Even if we don't do work on it.

00;11;00;21 - 00;11;22;13

It's not right. But if you know it, and that exists once the opportunity arrives, you already know it, then you can hit the ground running. Yeah. That's fantastic. So basically, as part of this learning journey, studying your cell phone, it's a party that you took part in. Definitely. Yeah. So what was your favorite aspect of that for week?

00;11;22;15 - 00;11;52;13

Yeah. I think, seeing the videos, making the quizzes, I was amazed with Joe. Yeah. Cool. Yeah. That's really cool. So did you manage to attend any of the videos live, or are you watching? Sort of, recording? Nope. I had done both. That's dedication. That's real dedication. The journey was only, to learn. We have, a few months just learning and doing things.

00;11;52;15 - 00;12;18;14

Yeah. Yeah. But in terms of, you know, what? What motivates you to keep doing. What is it? What's the secret? I think I'm a person that really wants to know more every time and keep searching and work for we can do. And, how can we automate our work? The aims, I think the I think it was all about that shit.

00;12;18;15 - 00;12;31;06

Oh, yeah. Yeah. How can we make our develop life better? Easier? And also curiosity driving. We got to learn. That's why I think that's the best definition of what developed. Yeah. Pedro, thanks very much. Thank you.

entry.title: Understanding SAP HANA Backup and Recovery - Technical Scope, Dependencies, and Design Boundaries
entry.content:
Introduction
SAP HANA provides a comprehensive and explicitly documented framework for backup and recovery. The core components of this framework are data backups, log backups, the backup catalog, and well-defined recovery procedures.

In many operational or architectural discussions, these components are treated as isolated features. From a technical perspective, however, recoverability is not a property of an individual backup. Recoverability emerges only from the complete and consistent backup chain over time, governed by strict dependencies that are explicitly documented by SAP.

This article consolidates SAP-documented SAP HANA backup and recovery mechanisms into a single coherent technical model.

Its objective is to make documented dependencies, scope, and design boundaries explicit, supporting sound architecture decisions, recovery validation planning, and technically correct discussions.

All statements in this article are derived from official SAP Help Portal and SAP HANA SQL Reference documentation.

1. Documented Core Components of SAP HANA Backup and Recovery
1.1 Data Backups
Data backups create a transaction-consistent image of the SAP HANA database at a defined point in time. SAP HANA supports different data backup types, such as full, differential, and incremental backups, each designed for specific operational and recovery scenarios.

A data backup establishes a single, consistent restore point.
However, a data backup does not define recoverability on its own.

From a technical perspective:

  • A data backup is a mandatory prerequisite for recovery.
  • Recoverability beyond the data backup timestamp depends on the availability of subsequent log backups and catalog metadata.

This distinction is explicitly reflected in SAP documentation.

SAP Help Portal reference
Backups (Overview)

Key technical boundary
A successful data backup defines a restore point, not continuous recoverability.

1.2 Log Backups
Log backups record all database changes that occur after a data backup. When log backup is enabled, SAP HANA generates log backups continuously.

From a recovery perspective, log backups are technically essential for:

  • recovery to the most recent state
  • point-in-time recovery

Log backups form a strictly sequential chain.
Recovery procedures that rely on log replay require this chain to be complete and uninterrupted.

If a required log backup segment is missing, corrupted, or no longer retained, recovery beyond that point is technically impossible, regardless of how many data backups exist.

SAP Help Portal reference
Log Backups

Key technical boundary
Log backups are not optional for recovery beyond a data backup timestamp.

1.3 Backup Catalog
The backup catalog stores metadata describing:

  • all data and log backups
  • their timestamps
  • their relationships and dependencies

SAP HANA uses the backup catalog to determine:

  • which backups exist
  • which backup chains are complete
  • which recovery targets are feasible

The backup catalog is therefore a technical dependency, not auxiliary metadata.

Even if backup files exist on disk or external storage, standard recovery procedures cannot be evaluated or executed without a consistent backup catalog.

SAP Help Portal reference
Backup Catalog

Additional technical detail:
What Information Is in the Backup Catalog?

Key technical boundary
Without a backup catalog, recoverability is limited and cannot be reliably determined.

2. Documented Recovery Capabilities and Scope
SAP HANA provides explicitly documented recovery procedures. The chosen recovery type determines which parts of the backup chain must be available.

2.1 Recovery to the Most Recent State
This recovery procedure restores the database to the latest possible point in time by:

  • restoring a suitable data backup
  • replaying all available log backups after that backup

Technical prerequisites:

  • a valid data backup
  • a complete and uninterrupted log backup chain
  • a consistent backup catalog

2.2 Point-in-Time Recovery
Point-in-time recovery restores the database to a specific timestamp.

From a technical standpoint:

  • all log backups covering the requested time range must be available
  • the backup catalog must describe the full chain

If required log backups for the selected interval are missing, recovery to that timestamp is not possible, even if older data backups exist.

SAP Help Portal reference
Point-In-Time Recovery of a Primary System

3. Recoverability as a Chain Property (Technical Dependency Model)
Recoverability is not a property of an individual backup artifact.

From a technical perspective, recovery depends on the combined availability of:

  • a suitable data backup
  • all required log backups following that backup
  • the corresponding backup catalog entries

SAP HANA tooling evaluates these dependencies using catalog-driven logic when determining feasible recovery options.

This behavior is explicitly reflected in the SAP HANA SQL Reference.

SAP HANA SQL Reference
BACKUP LIST DATA Statement

Documented boundary
Without a backup catalog, recovery capabilities are restricted

4. Retention and Recovery Window
Retention settings define how long data and log backups are kept.
From a technical standpoint, retention directly defines the recovery window.

If required log backups are no longer available:

  • recovery beyond that point is technically impossible
  • older data backups do not extend recoverability

This follows directly from SAP HANA’s log replay mechanics.

SAP Help Portal reference
Planning Your Backup and Recovery Strategy

5. Backup Operation Success vs. Recoverability
SAP HANA reports the success or failure of individual backup operations.

From a technical perspective:

  • backup success confirms that a single operation completed
  • recoverability is evaluated only at restore time

Recovery feasibility depends on:

  • chain completeness
  • catalog availability
  • chosen recovery target

Backup operations are events.
Recovery is a reconstruction procedure.
These are technically distinct concepts.

6. Separation from Availability Mechanisms
SAP HANA provides availability mechanisms such as system replication to reduce downtime. Backup and recovery address restoration to a previous state.

These mechanisms serve different documented objectives and must be designed and validated independently.

(Availability mechanisms are intentionally not covered in this article.)

Summary
SAP HANA provides all documented technical mechanisms required for reliable backup and recovery.

Recoverability is defined by the availability and consistency of the complete backup chain over time:

  • data backups
  • log backups
  • backup catalog

Understanding these documented dependencies and boundaries is essential for technically sound architecture design, realistic recovery expectations, and precise operational discussions.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud release January 2026 Round-up
entry.content:
Dear SAP HANA Cloud Enthusiasts,

We are thrilled to share a comprehensive collection of the latest videos, blogposts, and resources from the SAP HANA Cloud Q4 2025 release.

 

What’s New Teaser

Discover the newest advancements in SAP HANA Cloud with Lead Product Manager Thomas Hammer, as he highlights his top features from the latest release in this engaging teaser.

Watch it now on YouTube.

What’s New blogpost

Intrigued by the teaser? Explore our "What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud in December 2025" blogpost for an in-depth look at the innovations and find valuable links to further demos and content.

Read it here!

What's New Webinar

Prefer watching over reading? Our 'What’s New' webinar is just for you! Join our Product experts for an in-depth view of the latest features. Available to watch anytime here!

 

Don’t miss out on all the content and remember to follow us in the SAP HANA Community.

Remember to check our content following the # whatsnewinsaphanacloud tag: here

Don’t forget to subscribe and follow SAP HANA Cloud on YouTube to always stay up-to-date regarding the most recent innovations in SAP HANA Cloud.
 
All the best,

Andrea on behalf of the SAP HANA Cloud team

#SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database   #SAP HANA Cloud  

#whatsnewinsaphanacloud

entry.title: Code Connect 2026 is Coming – Mark Your Calendars!
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the return of Code Connect for its third edition, bringing together three dynamic events - UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con - under one roof. Mark your calendars for July 13 to 16, 2026, and join us in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, or online.

 

What is Code Connect?

Code Connect creates a unique opportunity to experience three specialized events in one location: UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con, allowing you to dive deep into different aspects of SAP development. Code Connect is designed for developers at every level: Whether you're an SAP veteran or just starting out, this is your chance to connect, learn, and innovate alongside a vibrant community of developers.

 

Your Week at Code Connect

July 13: Code Jam Sessions and Warmup

Kick things off with our Code Jam sessions - a hands-on way to sharpen your skills before the main event. Afterward, join us for a pre-conference networking event to meet fellow attendees in a relaxed setting.

 

 

 

July 14: UI5con

The official program kicks off with UI5con, bringing together UI5 enthusiasts to share insights, explore the latest innovations, and build new connections. Expect expert sessions, interactive workshops, and plenty of opportunities to engage with the UI5 community.

Learn more about UI5con.

 

 
 
 
 

July 15: reCAP

The next day focuses on the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). At reCAP, developers, customers, and partners meet the CAP Product Team to discuss technical concepts, share project experiences, and explore future possibilities.

Learn more about reCAP.

 

 

 

 

 

July 16: HANA Tech Con

The week concludes with HANA Tech Con. Delve into the HANA universe and join development experts, users, and partners to exchange knowledge and ignite new ideas. If you've ever had questions about HANA that you haven't found answers to, this is your chance to get them resolved.

Learn more about HANA Tech Con.

 

Early Bird Process

Planning a longer trip? We offer a limited number of early bird tickets for attendees who need to arrange travel well in advance.

Check our FAQ document for details on how to secure your ticket.

 

Sponsorship Opportunities

Be a part of Code Connect 2026 and become a sponsor to support the developer community at UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con. By sponsoring Code Connect, you gain access to a diverse audience spanning front-end developers, backend specialists, and database experts – all in one event.

Read our sponsorship prospectus to check our sponsorship opportunities.

 

Call for Proposals

Our Call for Speakers runs from 26 January to 13 March. As a speaker, you’ll be an active part of Code Connect, shaping the conversation, sharing your expertise, and inspiring the developer community. Don’t wait and submit your session proposal by 13 March at the latest.

  • Call for proposals UI5con
  • Call for proposals reCAP
  • Call for proposals HANA Tech Con

 

Important Dates

  • Call for Proposals: January 26, 2026 to March 13, 2026.
  • Registration Opens: April 10, 2026
  • Agenda Published: Early June 2026
  • Code Connect Week: July 13 to 16, 2026

 

Ready to Connect?

Code Connect 2026 represents more than just learning opportunities. It's about building relationships, sharing knowledge, and being part of a community that's shaping the future of SAP development.

Join us at Code Connect 2026 and be part of a community driving the future of technology. We can't wait to see you there!

entry.title: Transfer of medical instruments and implants in Medical device Industry using S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:  

This blog outlines the leading medical device industry business process steps to Transfer medical instruments and implants needed in a surgery from one branch to another branch and provide visibility, tracking, and logistic support to the business process using S4 HANA

In the field instruments and implants can be physically transferred from branch to another branch based on demand and utilization, this process needs to accurately be captured in the SAP system.

When a request is received for transfer of instrument and implants between branches (consignees), from Branch A to Branch B, The SAP data model requires the execution of two transactions; first, a Consignment Pickup Order that pulls the product back to warehouse stock in the “plant of origin” and purpose-built “transfer storage location” not visible to ATP from Branch A and immediately transfer to Branch B with transfer-specific Consignment Fill Order that moves the product to the new branch.

Pre-requisite:

  • The goods requested for transfer are consigned in the system against branch A
  • Virtual storage location set up in S4 to move stock between the rep and not visible to ATP
  • Hospitals and branches must be set up as customers
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials.

Setup Steps:

Configure new consignment document type: -

Step 1: Define Consignment fill up and pick up document types

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Define sales document type.

Consignment Pick up – To pick up inventory from branch A back to the warehouse and virtual storage location

Consignment Fill up. – To deliver inventory to sales rep B

Step 2: Define item categories.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item – Define item categories

Consignment pick-up item category

Consignment Fill-up Item category.

Step 3: Schedule lines

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines – Define schedule lines.

Consignment Pickup Schedule line.


Consignment Fill up the schedule line

Step 4: Assign document type to the sales area.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Assign Sales Area to Sales Document Types

Step 5: Assign Item Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item - Assign Item Categories

Step 6: Assign Schedule Line Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines - Assign Schedule Line Categories

Process steps: -

Step 1: - Create pick up order (KA) To pick up inventory from branch A

 


Step 2: Create Inbound delivery and receive goods in the warehouse of origin and virtual storage location.


Step 3. Create a Consignment fill up (KB) order transfer/deliver goods to branch B


Step 4: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue from the virtual storage location to rep B

Step 5: Asset updated with the new functional location

Conclusion:

  • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to transfer goods from one branch to another using S4 HANA
  • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

Reference:

 SAP Help Portal

Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Fulfillment of Trial request to the field in Medical Device Industry using S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:  

This blog outlines the leading industry business process steps to fulfill and track customer request for trials in the field providing visibility to all stakeholders.

It is common practice in the medical industry for customers to request trial of new or modified products to determine effectiveness before placing orders.

The trial products sent out from the warehouse to field must be tracked and stored appropriately when returned as these can be used across customers and geographies. This document details the fulfillment of trial products


Pre-requisite:

  • Hospitals must be set up as customers and sales representatives as employees
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials
  • Dedicated storage locations inside warehouses to store trial products
  1. Process Steps for Trial

 

Setup Steps:

Configure new consignment document type: -

Step 1: Define Consignment fill up and pick up document types

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Define sales document type.

Consignment Fill up. – To deliver trial inventory to customer


Consignment Pick up – To pick up inventory back to warehouse and dedicated storage location after trial


Step 2: Define item categories.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item – Define item categories

Consignment Fill-up Item category.

 

Consignment pick-up item category

Step 3: Schedule lines

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines – Define schedule lines.

Consignment Fill up the schedule line

 


Consignment Pickup Schedule line.


Step 4: Assign document type to the sales area.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Assign Sales Area to Sales Document Types

Step 5: Assign Item Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item - Assign Item Categories

Step 6: Assign Schedule Line Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines - Assign Schedule Line Categories

Process steps: -
Step 1. Create a Consignment fill up (KB) order send goods on trial from dedicated trial storage location


Step 2: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue from the virtual storage location to rep B

Step 4: Asset updated with the new functional location. Equipment identified as trial

Step 5: - Create pick up order (KA) To pick up inventory from customer location after trial


Step 2: Create Inbound delivery and receive goods in the warehouse of origin and virtual storage location.

 


Conclusion:

  • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to fulfill a customer request for trial of products using S4 HANA
  • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

Reference:

 SAP Help Portal

Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Expired Product Management in Medical Device Industry using S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:  

This blog outlines th leading medical device industry business process steps to return expired products from the field and to replenish for the depleted stocks due to return of expired products.

In the field instruments and implants can expire for various reasons, companies regularly monitor products on the field for expiration and pull them of the shelves and replenish them with new stocks. This process prevents expired products from reaching the customers.

Pre-requisite:

  • The goods due expiration in the field consigned to customers or branch
  • The materials are tracked using reports generated out of S4
  • Hospitals and branches must be set up as customers
  • Instruments and Consumables set up separate materials.

Process Flow:

 

Process Flow: Replenishment

Process Flow: Return Expired

 Setup Steps:

Configure new consignment document type: -

Step 1: Define Consignment fill up and pick up document types

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Define sales document type.

Consignment Pick up – To pick up inventory from branch A back to the warehouse and virtual storage location

Consignment Fill up. – To deliver inventory to sales rep B

Step 2: Define item categories.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item – Define item categories

Consignment pick-up item category

Consignment Fill-up Item category.

Step 3: Schedule lines

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines – Define schedule lines.

Consignment Pickup Schedule line.


Consignment Fill up the schedule line

Step 4: Assign document type to the sales area.

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document header – Assign Sales Area to Sales Document Types

Step 5: Assign Item Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Sales document item - Assign Item Categories

Step 6: Assign Schedule Line Categories

SPRO – Sales and Distribution – Sales document- Schedule lines - Assign Schedule Line Categories

Process steps: -

Step 1: - Create pick up order (KA) To pick up inventory from branch A


Step 2: Create Inbound delivery and receive goods in the warehouse of origin and virtual storage location.

Step 3. Create a Consignment fill up (KB) order transfer/deliver goods to branch B

Step 4: Create an outbound delivery with reference to the KB Order. Pick, pack, and Issue from the virtual storage location to rep B

Step 5: Asset updated with the new functional location


Conclusion:

  • After going through the above blog, the user should have learned how to pull back expired products from the field and replenish them with new stock in S4 HANA
  • Please provide your feedback/comments. You can ask Question / Answer for the open Questions in the SAP Community here

Reference:

 SAP Help Portal

Authors: Mohan Kaliyamoorthy and Immanuel Godwin Johnson

entry.title: Good to know: Analyzing Runtime Dump (SAP HANA Dump Analyzer vs Visual Studio Code)
entry.content:
• Why was this blog post created?
  • Where can I find the most important information about the SAP HANA Dump Analyzer and Visual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA?
  • What are the experiences based on the comparison of the tools?
  • Generated report inSAP HANA Dump Analyzer
  • Generated report inVisual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA
  • What is the conclusion?
  • Other articles
  • Do you have further questions?
  • Contribution

last updated: 2026-01-20

Why was this blog post created?

SAP HANA Dump Analyzer is a well-known and long-used tool. Same analysis can be done in Visual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA (SAP Extension). It's worth getting acquainted with the new tool.

Where can I find the most important information about the SAP HANA Dump Analyzer and Visual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA?

SAP Community Article: Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?

  • section Runtime Dump (RTE)
  • section SAP HANA Dump Analyzer

SAP Community Article: Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

  • section Visual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA
  • section SAP HANA Dump Analyzer

What are the experiences based on the comparison of the tools?

The same analysis can be performed with both tools. The presentation is slightly different, with Visual Studio Code providing a fresher, more modern look, but the information in the reports is exactly the same. If Visual Studio Code is already installed, you can access a very useful analytical tool, with just adding the necessary SAP extension.

 SAP HANA Dump AnalyzerVisual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA
DesignOld school.Modern.
Look and feelWell structured.Well structured but more compact.
What will be the generated result?.HTML fileResult will be opened in the application.
Can the various details and data be cut and copied?Yes.Yes.
Export.HTML fileN/A

Generated report in SAP HANA Dump Analyzer

The below example shows an outcome which generated via "Auto Analyzer" option. Other option is the "Expert Mode" - - > "Runtime Dump Mini Check". In some case there is a need to to generate both type of output for better understanding the issue.

Summary Info tab where system related information and detected issues presented.

In this example HANA Workload Analysis tab exists because this kind of issue has been identified.

Pie chart and highlighted statement hash gives us important information regarding the issue.

In this example Blocked Transactions tab exists because this kind of issue has been identified.

With Blocked Transaction wait Graph option we can get further details.

Generated report in Visual Studio Code - Supportability tools for SAP HANA

In Visual Studio Code we need to generate a work folder and import the files.

In this example High Workload tab exists because this kind of issue has been identified.

In this example Many Transactions Blocked tab exists because this kind of issue has been identified.

What is the conclusion?

Complete analysis can be performed using Visual Studio Code without installing the previously used SAP HANA Dump Analyzer. However, the old tool can be easily exported by saving it as a simple .html file, and with this action, the entire content can be stored offline and attached to an email, case, etc.

Other articles

✍️ Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?
✍️ Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

Do you have further questions?

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have question or observation regarding the article.
Q&A link for SAP HANA: https://answers.sap.com/tags/73554900100700000996 

Contribution

If you find any missing information belongs to the topic, please let me know. I am happy to add the new content. My intention is to maintain the content continuously to keep the info up-to-date.

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2026.01.20First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Technical Article).

entry.title: Creating value in airlines
entry.content:
MOST PROFITABLE, LARGEST, AND MOST VALUABLE ARILINE

As we travel back from the holidays, or into year-start meetings, it may be a good time to reflect on the companies that make such travel possible. In this case, let’s examine Delta Air Lines’ transformation journey with SAP.

Specifically, let us zoom in on three related areas of transformation:
1. Delta’s strategy of top customer experience, which among other things means on-time flights.
2. Workforce optimization to enable upward mobility for some 100,000 people.
3. Improving financial close, which was historically burdened with significant non-linearity.

These areas of Delta’s evolution show a clear connection between passenger experience, Delta’s own resources, and share- and debt-holders as providers of capital.

Let’s start from the customer excellence.
 
ON-TIME ARRIVAL
You can see the comparison of Delta with other major global airlines: horizontally plotted are percentages of flights with on-time arrival and vertically plotted are the number of flights per year. Delta, with some of the highest number of flights in the world, is constantly improving its on-time arrival statistics and currently stands at 84%. This is an amazing achievement for such a large operation. The accolades from the media and experts are well deserved.

But let’s look at what it took to achieve such operational excellence.

For now, let us focus on the processes part of the equation; we will talk about people soon.
The major reason for delayed flights, apart from the weather, is supplies. Catering, to be more specific. Before Delta embarked on the journey to significantly overhaul its operations, the state of the union was as follows:

  1. Very large operations across the globe… but email communications with the suppliers.
  2. Multiple items of supplies… but some not even recorded in Delta’s inventory system. For example, there were stories of half a million dollars' worth of earbuds and a million dollars' worth of blankets left in various airports without any visibility for Delta’s operational colleagues.

That had to change, and Delta did change them. To support its in-sourcing processes, the company adopted SAP IBP and SAP SAC to enable some 155 locations, 255 suppliers and 8 warehouses worldwide.
As a result of demand sensing and inventory optimization, Delta was able to optimize its catering, including the weight aspect of the supplies (for example, the number of food and beverage items based on the passenger count). This, in turn, led do significant fuel savings, which cumulatively accounted for some $100m cost savings in 2024.

To sum it up, for the first strategic transformation area, not only did Delta improve its on-time arrival KPI but it also removed some costs. This brings us to the people side of the equation…

Workforce mobility
Operational excellence goes hand in hand with the people excellence. Especially when you know that people expenses account for a quarter of your revenue or some $15 billion, and they grow faster than revenues.
The company decided to do a few things:
1. Lower the educational requirements and rather train people on the job.
2. Give people opportunities to advance.
3. Lower the “extra shift costs” where possible.

Why this was critical becomes clear when you look at Delta’s workforce…
Delta’s people represent a very diverse human resource organization, by location, type of job performed, and typical challenges.

So let us, together with Delta, look at one typical employee…
Like many large organizations, Delta sees two significant workforce drivers: Source & Recruit and Enable & Develop.
It’s the second one where the company realized it was missing a lot. Meet Eleanor, a typical high-achiever, who – through her effort on the ground – became a top performer (“red coat” in Delta’s language) but wants to push herself even higher. When Eleanor searched the corporate portal for job opportunities in Finance, some 1,100 results appeared – an impossible number to comprehend and take action on. This is what Delta’s colleagues call the “foggy gap”: the desire to learn and grow is present, but no enabler exists for that.

When the managers considered the traditional approach, they quickly realized its limitations and resulting frustration, both for the employees and for the hiring managers.

Therefore, Delta adopted its own, unique approach: FOCUS ON SKILLS, NOT JOB DESCRIPTION.

During the transformation, Delta created a repository of SKILLS making it available to employee and tying skills the Delta’s HR needs. This enables the employees to focus on what they either have to have to develop, as opposed to searching through numerous job postings and trying to decipher them.  The results of this approach are quite telling.  Half of the managerial positions are filled with experience Delta veterans ensuring upward mobility and job satisfaction, and the central skills repository is easy to maintain and update.

But most important is the overall outcome: employees who feel taken care of by Delta, take good care of the Delta customers.

Now that we have reviewed two strategic initiatives – on time flight arrival and employee mobility – we can look at another challenge Delta faced throughout the years.

 

Nonlinearity
It is January 1. Or April1. Time to close the year or quarter. Yet during this first week of January or April, Delta receives the vast majority of its invoices including from contractors for the previous period. The week is literally flooded with work. For the year close, we are talking about some $30 billion in gross debits! That’s half of the total annual business.

Such contracted services come from different sources, including companies such as Unifi, an approximately $2bn and 40,000 employee strong largest provider of air services in North America. (link)

Services such as plane taxying, cleaning, baggage loading, special needs passenger transportation, tie back to the on-time flight departure and arrival as well, so they are critical to Delta’s strategy...
…and for Delta, such services spell into some $4 billion cost item, i.e., approximately $1bn each quarter.

Before the transformation, most of such reconciliation and postings were manual. To automate this process, Delta utilized SAP HANA as the main source of flight data, coupled it with the AI tools from Blackline, creating a more automatic incoming invoice matching and posting process.

As a result, some 2/3 ds of the accruals became automatic matches, and that trend continues. Overall, this automation significantly lessens the strain of period close and compresses the time to close, freeing oCFO colleagues’ time for higher added value work.

But the amazing by-product of the automation is a much shorter time to publish quarterly reports. Among the major US airlines, Delta is the fastest to file its 10-Qs, the quarterly reports, therefore improving its stance with the share- and debt-holders.

So, the question is, does the market notice all these Delta’s efforts to evolve across the three strategic imperatives?

Recap
The answer is unequivocal yes. Through its incredible journey to improve customer service, create workforce mobility, and combat non-linearity for faster financial close, in the past 5 years, Delta grew its Enterprise Value by 1.4x or at approximately 7.6% CAGR. Delta has become one of the most profitable and the most valuable airline.  I am proud to say that SAP has supported Delta in every step.


*    *     *

Big debt of gratitude to my partners at SAP and, most importantly, to Delta – for being an SAP customer!
